---
title: "Programming Practice: Lab 5"
execute:
  enabled: true
  echo: false
  cache: true
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
jupyter: python3
diagram:
  cache: true
  cache-dir: ./cache
  engine:
    tikz:
      execpath: lualatex
filters:
  - diagram
---

# SQL part 3

Now we can integrate SQL work and BlockProcessor.

## Exercise 1
Modify BlockProcessor code so that it uses SQLite database instead of CSVs.

1. Create an extra Python script `updater` that will be doing the insertions. This should run as a console app, that can either read csv files in bulk, or listen for user input.
2. `updater` will insert records into `Blocks`/`Votes`/`Persons`/`Sources`, and will also update a new table - `event_stream`, containing just two fields - `type` and `id`.
3. Actual `BlockProcessor` will check the `event_stream` table for updates periodically (usi, look into respective tables (`blocks` or `votes`), and invoke chain processing code.

:::{.callout-important}
Use some kind of mechanism to mark records in `event_stream` as read by `BlockProcessor`.
:::
